title: Climate Solutions Tracker Update — Wed Apr 29, 2026
summary: The First International Conference on Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels concluded in Santa Marta, Colombia with 56 nations committing to a follow-up summit hosted by Tuvalu. A 53-country 'highest ambition coalition' finalized demands for a binding fossil fuel phase-out treaty. Global clean energy investment rose to a record $2.3 trillion in 2025 (up 10% vs 2024). The IEA's Global Energy Review 2026 confirmed solar led world energy demand growth for the first time in history (released April 20). The US EIA forecasts 80+ GW of new solar, wind, and battery storage added to the US grid by early 2027.

title: Climate Solutions Tracker Update — Tue Apr 28, 2026
summary: Santa Marta Summit: 53-Nation Coalition Calls for Legally Binding Fossil Fuel Phase-Out Instrument.

title: Climate Solutions Tracker Update — Mon Apr 27, 2026
summary: Colombia: Nations Backing Fossil Fuel Exit Are a 'New Power' in Global Climate Diplomacy.

title: Climate Solutions Tracker Update — Sun Apr 26, 2026
summary: Amid persistently high global oil prices driven by Middle East tensions, nations accelerated their clean energy procurement in early 2026. Oxfam released a major report on April 26 showing six major fossil fuel companies are projected to earn approximately $2,967 per second in profits in 2026, totaling tens of billions annually.

title: Climate Solutions Tracker Update — Thu Apr 23, 2026
summary: Climate Change News reported on April 23 that over 60 countries had confirmed attendance at the First Conference on Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels, opening April 24 in Santa Marta, Colombia.

title: Climate Solutions Tracker Update — Wed Apr 22, 2026
summary: Earth Day 2026 on April 22 adopted the theme 'Our Power, Our Planet,' centering the global environmental movement on urgent acceleration of renewable energy. EU Unveils AccelerateEU: €660B/yr Clean Energy Plan to End Fossil Dependence.

title: Climate Solutions Tracker Update — Tue Apr 21, 2026
summary: Ember: Renewables Overtook Coal Globally for First Time Since 1919.

title: Climate Solutions Update — Apr 20, 2026
summary: Solar overtook all other energy technologies as the world's #1 source of energy supply growth in 2025, with 600 TWh added — a global first confirmed by the IEA on April 19. IRENA reported a record 692 GW of renewables added in 2025, bringing the global total to 5,149 GW (nearly 50% of all power capacity), with solar hitting 2,392 GW cumulative and wind adding 159 GW. Global EV sales exceeded 20 million units — 1 in 4 new cars sold. The EU's 2040 Climate Law (90% emissions cut by 2040) entered into force on April 7. On the downside, Microsoft's pause on carbon removal purchases raised concern for the DAC industry it largely underpins.

title: Climate Solutions Tracker Update — Sun Apr 19, 2026
summary: IEA: Solar Overtakes All Other Energy Sources in Global Growth for First Time in History.
